Our Products

ERP-integrated products for enterprise operations

Each product extends ERP workflows into real-world execution environments — without modifying ERP core. All products integrate with SAP Business One, SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP, and Epicor.

ERP-Integrated CRM

Synchronize sales opportunities, quotations, pricing, credit limits, and customer master data directly with SAP and enterprise ERP systems. Eliminate duplicate entry, improve sales visibility, and enable field reps to create ERP-governed orders from mobile devices — online or offline.
Learn more

ERP-Integrated Field Service Management

Manage work orders, assets, warranties, and technician workflows with mobile execution connected to ERP financial and service modules. Real-time work order dispatch, offline job capture, spare parts tracking, and automated SAP updates — without modifying ERP core.
Learn more

Warehouse & Inventory

Mobile inventory audits, goods receipt, inter-warehouse transfers, and batch tracking with real-time ERP synchronization. Barcode and QR scanning, bin-level control, and automated stock reconciliation keep warehouse operations accurate and audit-ready at every stage.
Learn more

Sales & Distribution Execution

Route-to-market with ERP-integrated order booking, van sales POS, distributor management, and secondary sales visibility. Field reps capture orders, collections, and returns on mobile — synced instantly to ERP without manual re-entry or data lag.
Learn more

Manufacturing & Shopfloor

Production updates, maintenance records, and operational data at the shopfloor while preserving ERP as the financial backbone. Capture production completions, downtime events, quality checks, and material consumption in real time — feeding directly into ERP without manual intervention.
Learn more

Enterprise & Partner Portals

Secure digital portals for suppliers, dealers, and partners — connected directly to ERP master data and transactional workflows. Vendors submit invoices, dealers place orders, and distributors track deliveries through branded self-service portals with live ERP data behind every interaction.
Learn more

ERP-Integrated Product Configurator

Configure products, pricing, and variants in real time with seamless ERP and SAP integration. Sales teams build accurate, rule-driven quotes with ERP-validated pricing, BOMs, and availability — eliminating configuration errors before orders ever reach production.
Learn more

Asset Tracking System

Real-time asset visibility, IoT-enabled preventive maintenance, barcode/QR/RFID lifecycle management, and audit automation — integrated with SAP Business One, SAP S/4HANA, Oracle, and Epicor without modifying ERP core. Track every asset from procurement to disposal with full depreciation, compliance, and maintenance history.
Learn more

Seamless ERP connectivity — agnostic by design

UpBuff integrates with every major enterprise ERP platform

UpBuff is ERP-agnostic by design — integrating securely using ERP-native APIs and service layers. SAP Business One via Service Layer, SAP S/4HANA via OData and RFC, Oracle ERP via REST, and Epicor via flexible API connectivity. No direct database writes. No core modifications. ERP governance preserved at every layer.

About UpBuff

Our story

In 2017, a founder and a handful of technology passionates set out to solve a problem that every enterprise running SAP already lived with — the gap between what ERP recorded and what operations teams could actually execute in the field.

What started as a single service management solution has grown into an ERP Execution Platform trusted by operations teams across five industries — all without ever modifying an ERP core.

We listen. We challenge.
We work collaboratively.
We make things.

UpBuff makes it fast and smooth for businesses to satisfy their users, customers, employees, and mentors. We adopted a fresh approach to building and delivering software-as-a-service that’s affordable, quick to implement, and designed for the end-user.

Where It All Began

In 2017, under the leadership of our founder Mr. Shiburaj Krishnapillai and cofounder Mr. Rajesh Kattilepurath, a small team of deeply committed technologists launched a service management solution that created a groundbreaking impact for their first client.

What started as a focused field service tool was gradually revamped — expanded with updated architecture, new modules, and deeper ERP integrations — delivering better customer experiences with every iteration and laying the foundation for what UpBuff would become.

Building the Faith

We adopted one formula for earning trust — and only one: build relationships that last. Over half a decade, that approach scaled our products, our team, and our reputation across some of the world’s most critical businesses, governments, and organisations.

Trust is a valuable commodity. Relationships are gold. Those two beliefs have never changed from the first deployment in 2017 to every enterprise we run today — and they never will.

What It Looks Like in Practice

Field technicians at a drone manufacturer logging service orders from a jobsite in Rajasthan. Warehouse operators at a chemical distributor scanning goods receipts that sync to SAP in under a second. Van sales reps capturing orders offline and pushing them to ERP the moment connectivity returns.

Every one of those deployments runs on UpBuff. Every one of them runs without a single modification to ERP core. And every one of them survived an ERP upgrade without a day of re-testing.

“

Our team spans ERP architects, mobile engineers, and integration specialists across India and beyond — but our commitment has never changed:every enterprise deserves execution speed without paying for it with ERP instability.

The market does not need another standalone app. It needs a platform thatspeaks the language of ERP — in real time, without compromise, and without ever asking IT to touch the core.

Our Vision

A world where “we had to customise ERP” is never the answer to an operational problem. Where every enterprise can extend its ERP capabilities safely — without modification risk, without upgrade anxiety, and without trading long-term platform health for short-term speed. Clean Core is not a constraint. It is the only sustainable foundation for enterprise technology.

Our Mission

To build the ERP Execution Platform that eliminates the false choice between operational agility and ERP stability — by connecting field teams, warehouse operators, and sales reps to live ERP data through official APIs, with zero core modifications, full audit traceability, and an architecture that stays upgrade-safe for the lifetime of your ERP investment.

Join our team

We are a small team solving a large problem. If you understand enterprise ERP, care deeply about clean software architecture, and want to build tools that real operations teams use every day — we want to hear from you.

See open roles

Our Engineering Culture

Six commitments that govern how we build, ship, and stand behind the UpBuff platform.

01

Clean Core is non-negotiable

No feature ships if it requires a core ERP modification to work. That constraint is not a limitation — it is the reason our customers can upgrade their ERP without fear.

02

Build fast. Break nothing.

Velocity matters. But speed that creates instability is not velocity — it is cost deferred. We move fast within a discipline that keeps every deployment stable long after go-live.

03

Validate in the field, not in a demo

Features are tested in live ERP environments against real operational workflows. If it does not hold up under field conditions, it does not ship. No exceptions.

04

Document the hard parts loudly

Enterprise integration is complex. We do not hide that complexity behind marketing language. We document the edge cases, the failure modes, and the gotchas — for our team and for our customers.

05

Integration is a team sport

Engineers, implementation specialists, and customer success work as one unit on every deployment. There is no handoff culture at UpBuff — the team that builds it owns it in production.

06

Publish what you learn

We invest in architecture guides, integration patterns, and technical content — because the enterprise software community is better when knowledge circulates rather than accumulates in private.

FAQs

About UpBuff — questions answered

A Clean Core ERP strategy means all extensions and integrations happen through official ERP APIs — never through direct database writes or core modifications. This keeps ERP stable, auditable, and safe for every future upgrade cycle. It is the architectural foundation every UpBuff deployment is built on.

No. While we have the deepest expertise in SAP Business One — including Service Layer, DI API, and Crystal Reports integrations — UpBuff also integrates with SAP S/4HANA via OData and BAPIs, Oracle ERP Cloud, Oracle NetSuite, and Epicor Kinetic via REST. Every integration uses official APIs only.

An ERP Execution Platform is a dedicated layer that sits outside your ERP and handles the operational execution your ERP was never designed to do alone — mobile field workflows, warehouse scanning, van sales, shopfloor management. ERP stays the system of record. UpBuff becomes the system of execution. The two talk to each other through official APIs in real time.

Because every ERP modification you make today becomes a re-testing obligation in your next upgrade. Enterprises that customise ERP cores spend an average of 18 to 24 months on S/4HANA migrations that should take 6. UpBuff customers complete the same upgrades faster because there is nothing to re-test — the execution layer was never inside ERP to begin with.

See what Clean Core execution looks like in your ERP.

Book a session with our integration team — we will map UpBuff to your specific ERP environment and show you exactly how it works without touching your core.

SAP customization blocks industry execution

Shopfloor & field teams lack mobile interfaces

SAP screens were designed for desk-based users. Operators, technicians, and field reps work from mobile devices in conditions where ERP complexity is a liability, not an asset.

Custom SAP modifications block upgrades

Every customization inside SAP becomes a regression risk at upgrade time. Routine upgrade cycles become multi-month re-implementation projects with significant consultant cost.

Industry operations need speed — ERP needs stability

Shopfloor confirmations, warehouse scans, and field service updates happen in real time. ERP governance requirements mean these workflows cannot live safely inside a customized SAP core.

Scaling to new sites means replicating fragile custom code

Custom SAP configurations do not scale cleanly across multiple plants, warehouses, or territories — each new location inherits the same technical debt and fragility.

The Problem

Traditional SAP Business One extensions create fragile architecture

Every third-party add-on installed inside SAP Business One becomes a compatibility liability. SAP version upgrades require each add-on to be re-certified, re-tested, and often re-purchased — turning routine upgrades into months-long projects.

DI-API overuse creates instability

The DI-API was designed for specific programmatic operations — not for high-frequency operational workflows. Overusing it for warehouse scanning, field service updates, and shopfloor confirmations creates performance bottlenecks and stability risks.

Core modifications destroy upgrade safety

Customizations made directly inside SAP Business One core — custom objects, modified UDFs, altered business logic — must be manually migrated or rebuilt at every major version upgrade. The cost compounds with each release cycle.

Data Protection | UpBuff Technologies — UpBuff

Security

Data Protection

Your data, our responsibility.

Last updated:01 April 2026

At UpBuff, securing and protecting your data is a prime responsibility — not an afterthought. Every layer of our product architecture, every deployment decision, and every access control exists to ensure your data remains yours: private, secure, and under your control at all times. The following describes the security practices, controls, and standards we maintain across the UpBuff platform.

Architecture

Five-layer security protection

UpBuff products are built on a five-layer security model. Each layer addresses a distinct attack surface — from encrypted transit at the network edge to governed API boundaries at the integration layer. No single layer is sufficient on its own; all five work together to provide defence in depth.

01
Secure Socket Layer (SSL)

All data in transit is encrypted via SSL/TLS, providing an essential barrier against interception and man-in-the-middle attacks across every connection to the UpBuff platform.

02
Cloud Firewall Protection

Infrastructure hosted on AWS, Microsoft Azure, and DigitalOcean is protected by cloud-native firewalls that monitor and block network-level intrusions before they reach application layers.

03
Web Application Firewall (WAF)

A dedicated WAF monitors all incoming traffic, filters malicious IPs, blocks spam and injection attacks, and protects application endpoints from exploitation at the HTTP layer.

04
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

Every user is granted access only to the data and functions their role requires. Administrators enforce industry-standard password policies, and access privileges are reviewed on a regular cadence.

05
API Security Framework

All integrations with third-party platforms — including SAP, Salesforce, and WhatsApp — are mediated through a secure API framework that validates, parses, and governs data exchange at every boundary.

Customer data isolation

UpBuff adheres to a cloud data model in which each customer has their own dedicated database — ensuring that data retrieved at any point belongs exclusively to that customer. Individual customers can access only their own data. All data is secured, controlled, and maintained with access logging for subsequent audit by the UpBuff team.

Controls

Security controls in practice

Product security

UpBuff cloud platforms are maintained across multiple regions on AWS, Microsoft Azure, and DigitalOcean. Our integrated architectural approach ensures compliance from engineering to deployment, with cloud security built into every stage of the delivery pipeline.

Data security

All customer data is encrypted in transit over SSL-secured connections for every individual account. Access to production servers is restricted to authorised UpBuff resources only, enforced through multi-factor authentication at every entry point.

Network security

The UpBuff network is monitored by industry-grade firewalls. Production and testing environments are hosted on SSL-certified servers across AWS, DigitalOcean, and Azure. Remote access to production servers is restricted exclusively to secured office networks.

Change Management

Changes, merging and release requests

Our internal change control process diligently tracks all application, infrastructure, and content modifications. UpBuff conducts routine deployment reviews on a quarterly basis, with oversight and direction provided by senior management.

Access to development environments is strictly governed and limited solely to the authorised UpBuff team. This bars any access from the customer side, ensuring an environment that effectively prevents potential malfunctions and maintains the integrity of production systems.

Data Lifecycle

Data deletion

15-day guaranteed deletion

If you withdraw from our service or terminate your account, we will transfer all your data to you and permanently delete all data from UpBuff servers within15 daysof account closure. No residual data is retained beyond this window unless required by applicable law.

Responsible Disclosure

Responding to issues and threats

If you discover any issues or potential threats to the security or privacy of UpBuff, please reach out to us promptly at:

security@upbuff.com

Your cooperation is vital in addressing security concerns promptly and effectively. We kindly request that you refrain from disclosing any unresolved vulnerabilities in public domains to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of our security efforts.

We greatly value your assistance in identifying and rectifying vulnerabilities within UpBuff products. Your efforts do not go unnoticed — we will formally recognise your contribution as soon as the issue has been successfully mitigated.

Public Non-Disclosure Policy

This programme does not allow public disclosure. Any individual who posts or publicly releases a vulnerability discovered through this programme shall be liable for legal penalties under applicable law.

The fine print

UpBuff may modify the terms of or terminate this programme at any time.

We will not apply any changes to these programme terms retroactively.

UpBuff employees and their immediate family members are not eligible for any bounties or recognition rewards under this programme.

Contact us

For security-related enquiries or to report a vulnerability, contact our security team:

UpBuff Technologies Pvt. Ltd., 5th Floor, CDAC, Technopark, Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India – 695582

security@upbuff.com

At UpBuff, safeguarding your data privacy is our paramount commitment. The trust between you — the user — and UpBuff forms the bedrock of our relationship. Central to fostering this trust is our dedication to transparently explaining how we use your data and uphold your privacy rights.

Definitions

What these terms mean

This policy describes howUpBuff Technologies Pvt. Ltd.(“UpBuff”, “Company”, “we”, “us”, or “our”) collects, protects, and uses personally identifiable information (“Personal Information” or “Personal Data”) you (“Customer”, “you”, or “your”) may provide through our website or any of its products or services (collectively, “Website”, “Products”, or “Application”).

It also describes the choices available to you regarding our use of your Personal Information and how you can access and update this information. This Policy does not apply to the practices of third-party companies that we do not own or control, or to individuals that we do not employ or manage.

Section 1

UpBuff Platform Overview | ERP Execution Layer for SAP & Enterprise ERP — UpBuff

The ERP Execution Layer for Enterprise Operations

Extend SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business One, Oracle, and Epicor into real-time field, warehouse, sales, and shopfloor operations — without modifying ERP core.

Explore platform capabilities

U

Clean Core

API-first

Upgrade-safe

Powering execution across leading enterprise

Platform Overview

ERP stays clean.

Execution stays fast.

Turn your ERP from a system of record into a system of action

UpBuff sits outside ERP core — connecting via official APIs (SAP Service Layer, OData, BAPI, REST) to deliver real-time operational execution for field, warehouse, sales, and shopfloor teams. No database writes. No core modifications. No upgrade risk.

“UpBuff gave us the execution layer we needed without touching SAP core. Our S/4HANA upgrade timeline went from 18 months to 6 months as a direct result.”

No direct database writes

All operations flow through official ERP API endpoints — keeping your ERP pristine for every future upgrade.

Real-time ERP synchronization

Every field action, warehouse scan, and sales order syncs back to ERP instantly via secured API channels.

Clean Core by design

Aligns with SAP Clean Core and Oracle Cloud best practices — upgrade-safe and audit-ready from day one.

The Problem

ERP customization creates compounding long-term risk

Most enterprises extend ERP the wrong way — embedding operational workflows into ERP core. As operational complexity grows, that decision becomes increasingly costly, fragile, and hard to reverse.

Upgrade delays & technical debt

Every customization embedded in ERP core must be re-tested at each upgrade cycle — turning routine upgrades into expensive multi-month projects.

Consultant dependency

Bespoke ERP modifications create reliance on niche consultants to maintain, debug, and extend logic that nobody else fully understands.

Compliance & audit gaps

Undocumented ERP modifications break audit trails and introduce regulatory exposure that is hard to detect and even harder to remediate.

Operational teams left behind

ERP interfaces were designed for administrators — not field technicians, warehouse operators, or production supervisors working at speed.

Architecture

Three pillars of the UpBuff execution layer

Every design decision in UpBuff is built on three architectural commitments — Clean Core compliance, mobile-first execution, and enterprise-grade security.

SAP · Oracle · Epicor

System of Record · No core mods

Clean Core — ERP stays the system of record

UpBuff never writes to ERP database tables directly. All data flows through SAP Service Layer, OData, BAPI, or REST — keeping ERP clean, governable, and upgrade-safe at every layer.

Field · Warehouse · Shopfloor · Sales

Offline-capable · Real-time sync

Mobile-first — UpBuff is the system of execution

Field technicians, warehouse operators, production supervisors, and sales reps get purpose-built mobile interfaces — fast, offline-capable, and built for their operational reality.

TLS encrypted · RBAC · Audit logs

SOC 2 · GDPR compliant

Enterprise security — audit-ready by architecture

Encrypted API communication, role-based access control mapped to ERP user roles, and full transaction audit logging — without the security gaps that come with undocumented ERP modifications.

Capabilities

What the UpBuff platform delivers

Six execution capabilities — all designed to extend ERP into frontline operations without ever touching ERP core.

Configurable Workflow Engine

Build execution workflows for manufacturing, warehouse, field service, and sales — all synchronized to ERP in real time.

Mobile-First Interfaces

Purpose-built mobile apps for field technicians, warehouse operators, production supervisors, and sales reps.

Real-Time ERP Synchronization

Every transaction validated, processed, and synced via official ERP APIs with full audit logging at every step.

Offline-First Resilience

Field and warehouse teams capture data without connectivity. Secure sync resumes automatically when connection returns.

Clean Core Compliance

ERP upgrades remain predictable and low-risk. Custom code inside ERP is minimized — not compounded.

Full Audit Traceability

Every action logged with user identifiers, timestamps, and transaction references — mapped to ERP user roles.

Products

One platform across every operational domain

UpBuff delivers a unified execution layer across the full enterprise operational stack — each module purpose-built for its domain, all sharing the same Clean Core ERP integration backbone.

ERP-Integrated CRM

Extend SAP into real-time field sales. Mobile order capture, ERP pricing enforcement, credit control, and offline sync — enabling sales teams to operate at ERP speed without ERP complexity. Reduce manual data entry by up to 40% and accelerate order-to-cash cycles without touching SAP core.
Learn more

ERP-Integrated Field Service Management

Connect SAP work orders, spare parts, and service records to field technicians in real time. Offline-first mobile execution, intelligent dispatch, digital sign-offs, and automated SAP updates — without any ERP core modification or upgrade risk.
Learn more

Warehouse & Inventory

Barcode-driven warehouse operations synchronized with SAP inventory — goods receipt, bin transfers, pick-pack-ship, and cycle counts in real time. Full audit trail from scan to ERP posting without manual re-entry.
Learn more

Manufacturing & Shopfloor

Production orders, BOM confirmations, quality checks, and shopfloor reporting directly connected to SAP PM and PP — eliminating paper-based tracking and syncing every operation to ERP in real time.
Learn more

Sales & Distribution Execution

Distributor order management, secondary sales, channel partner portals, and collections — all governed by ERP pricing, credit, and tax rules with real-time synchronization back to SAP.
Learn more

Enterprise & Partner Portals

Secure digital portals for suppliers, dealers, and partners — connected directly to ERP master data and transactional workflows. Self-service access with live ERP data behind every interaction.
Learn more

ERP-Integrated Product Configurator

Sales teams build accurate, rule-driven quotes with ERP-validated pricing, BOMs, and availability — eliminating configuration errors before orders ever reach production.
Learn more

Asset Tracking System

Real-time asset visibility, IoT-enabled preventive maintenance, barcode/QR/RFID lifecycle management, and audit automation — integrated with SAP, Oracle, and Epicor without modifying ERP core.
Learn more

Design Principles

Built to protect your ERP investment

Every technical decision in UpBuff preserves ERP stability, reduces long-term risk, and enables operational agility without architectural compromise.

Upgrade-safe by design

No direct database manipulation — ever

UpBuff never writes to ERP database tables or bypasses official API layers. All operations flow through SAP Service Layer, OData, BAPI, or REST — keeping ERP pristine for every future upgrade cycle.

API-first integration

Role-based access mapped to ERP user roles

Every user, transaction, and data element is governed by enterprise-grade RBAC — mapped directly to ERP user roles and organizational structures. No access exists outside ERP governance.

Full audit traceability

TLS encryption & complete transaction audit logs

All data in transit is encrypted with TLS 1.3. Every action is timestamped, logged, and auditable — providing compliance teams with full traceability without disrupting operational workflows.

Data integrity assurance

Real-time SAP data consistency across all systems

Ensure real-time synchronization of master data, transactions, and documents across SAP ERP and connected applications. UpBuff eliminates data duplication, inconsistencies, and reconciliation issues in enterprise workflows.

Enterprise scalability

Scalable architecture for high-volume ERP transactions

Designed for enterprise-grade scalability, UpBuff supports high transaction volumes, multi-user environments, and distributed operations — ensuring reliable SAP integration performance without system bottlenecks.

Enterprise

What's included in Enterprise

✓SAP integration live

Dedicated ERP integration support

A dedicated integration engineer handles your SAP Service Layer, OData, or BAPI connection — from initial setup through go-live and beyond.

Custom ERP workflow configuration

We build and configure execution workflows tailored to your ERP landscape — SAP Business One, S/4HANA, Oracle, or Epicor — with full testing and documentation.

99.9%

SLA guarantees & uptime commitments

Enterprise-grade uptime SLAs, priority support queues, and dedicated monitoring — backed by contractual commitments, not just best-effort promises.

Connects your entire enterprise ecosystem

UpBuff bridges ERP with the operational tools, communication platforms, and business systems your teams already rely on — without disrupting ERP governance or data integrity.

Security & Compliance

Enterprise-grade security

built into the architecture

UpBuff is SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant. Security is not a feature added on top — it is a foundational architectural commitment that governs every integration, every API call, and every data transaction.

ERP data stays in ERP

UpBuff never stores ERP master data externally. Your SAP or Oracle data is accessed via scoped API calls and never duplicated outside your ERP boundary.

TLS encryption & secret management

All data in transit is encrypted with TLS 1.3. API credentials are stored with industry-standard secret management — never exposed in application code or logs.

RBAC & full audit trail

Granular role-based access control mapped to ERP user roles. Every transaction logged with timestamps and user identifiers — full audit traceability for compliance teams.

What enterprise teams say

From SAP Business One customers to large-scale S/4HANA environments — here's what leaders say about adopting a Clean Core execution layer

"UpBuff gave us the execution layer we needed without any SAP core modifications. Our upgrade timeline dropped from 18 months to 6. That single architectural decision paid for itself in the first cycle."

Enterprise Architect

National Grid

"The Clean Core approach was exactly what our CIO mandated. UpBuff delivered real-time field execution while keeping our SAP S/4HANA upgrade path completely intact and on schedule."

Head of Digital Operations

Miele

10,000+

enterprise users on the UpBuff execution platform

"We evaluated five ERP extension platforms. UpBuff was the only one that truly respected our SAP governance model — API-first, role-controlled, and fully auditable from day one."

CIO

IHG Hotels & Resorts

"Deploying UpBuff across hybrid cloud and on-premise SAP environments simultaneously was seamless. No compromise in ERP data integrity or compliance posture — exactly what we needed."

IT Director

Opal

FAQs

Frequently asked questions

An ERP Execution Platform extends SAP, Oracle, or Epicor ERP into real-time frontline operations — field service, warehouse, sales, and shopfloor — via official APIs without modifying ERP core. ERP stays the system of record; UpBuff becomes the system of execution.

No. ERP remains the system of record for all master data, financials, inventory, and transactions. UpBuff handles real-time execution workflows and synchronizes every outcome back to ERP via official APIs after each operation.

Yes. UpBuff follows SAP Clean Core principles and integrates exclusively via official SAP APIs — SAP Service Layer, OData, and BAPI. This makes it fully compatible with S/4HANA upgrade strategies, cloud transitions, and ECC migration roadmaps.

UpBuff integrates with SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, SAP Business One, Epicor ERP, and Oracle ERP — across on-premise, private cloud, public cloud, and hybrid deployment models.

Yes. UpBuff supports full offline operation for field and warehouse teams. Data is captured locally and synced securely to ERP when connectivity is restored — with no data loss and full conflict resolution.

Standard integrations with SAP Business One or S/4HANA are typically live in 4–8 weeks. Complex multi-instance or multi-ERP deployments are scoped individually with dedicated integration engineering support.

Ready to modernize ERP execution without customization risk?

Extend SAP, Oracle, and Epicor into real-time frontline operations — without touching ERP core, disrupting upgrade roadmaps, or building technical debt.

Request a Platform Demo

No ERP core modifications required

Security & Compliance | UpBuff ERP Execution Platform — UpBuff

Quick Connect+91 83300 06006
Contact Sales

Security & Compliance

Security is architecture, not a feature added on top.

UpBuff is designed for enterprise security, compliance, and data governance at scale. Every security control is built into the platform architecture, not bolted on after deployment. Because ERP data deserves enterprise-grade protection at every layer, every API call, and every transaction.

Request Security ReviewDownload Security Overview

Security Posture — Live Status

TLS 1.3 — All connections encrypted

Role-Based Access — ERP governed

Audit Logs — Tamper-evident

Zero ERP data stored externally

Security Controls

End-to-End TLS Encryption

Transport layer · TLS 1.3

Role-Based Access Control

Mapped to ERP user roles

Secret & Credential Vault

Auto-rotation · No plaintext

Transaction Audit Logs

Tamper-evident · Exportable

ERP Data Boundary Enforcement

API-only · No external store

Enterprise-Grade Security for SAP and Enterprise ERP Environments

Security Philosophy

Built secure from the ground up — not secured after the fact

Every design decision starts with security

Most platforms add security features after the product is built. UpBuff is different — security is the foundation on which every feature is designed. From the first API call to the final ERP posting, every action is encrypted, authenticated, authorized, and logged. There are no exceptions.

“UpBuff was the only ERP extension platform that could satisfy our enterprise security review. API-first, fully auditable, and role-controlled — it passed every checkpoint our InfoSec team set.”

TLS 1.3 encryption in transit

All data moving between UpBuff, ERP systems, and mobile clients is encrypted with TLS 1.3.

API-only ERP integration

No direct database writes. All ERP integration flows through official APIs — SAP Service Layer, OData, BAPI. Upgrade-safe and audit-clean.

Full transaction audit trail

Every action is logged with timestamp, user ID, and transaction reference — nothing is anonymous.

Core Security

Six foundational security capabilities

Every capability listed here is built into the platform architecture — not available as an optional add-on or enterprise-tier upgrade.

End-to-End TLS Encryption

All data in transit between UpBuff, ERP systems, mobile clients, and third-party integrations uses TLS 1.3 — the current industry gold standard for transport encryption.

Secret & Credential Management

API keys, ERP service credentials, and integration secrets are stored using vault-based secret management — never in application code, config files, or logs.

Role-Based Access Control

Granular RBAC mapped directly to ERP user roles and organizational structures. Field reps see only their territory. Warehouse operators access only their warehouse. No permission exists outside ERP governance.

Full Transaction Audit Logs

Every user action, API call, data access, and ERP transaction is logged with timestamp, user identifier, session context, and transaction reference — providing complete audit traceability.

Multi-Level Approval Workflows

Configurable approval chains for credit, pricing, discounts, and order authorization — integrated with ERP business logic to ensure no transaction bypasses governance controls.

ERP Data Boundary Enforcement

UpBuff never stores ERP master data externally. All data is accessed via scoped API calls and never duplicated outside your ERP boundary — preserving data sovereignty and governance.

Security Layers

Three layers of enterprise security

UpBuff security is structured in three independent layers — each protecting a different surface area of the ERP integration. A vulnerability at one layer is isolated and cannot cascade to the others.

UpBuffClient

TLS 1.3

SAP ERPSystem

ODataBAPIService LayerNo direct DB

Layer 1 — API & Transport Security

All communication between UpBuff and ERP systems flows through official API endpoints — SAP Service Layer, OData, BAPI, REST. TLS 1.3 encryption on every request. No direct database access. No custom transport protocols. Certificate pinning on mobile clients prevents man-in-the-middle attacks.

AuthenticateMFA verified

Check ERP roleRBAC mapped

Issue scoped tokenAccess granted

Layer 2 — Identity & Access Control

Every user, every session, and every permission is authenticated and authorized before any ERP data is accessed. RBAC mapped to ERP roles. Multi-factor authentication supported. Session tokens are short-lived, scoped, and automatically rotated. No shared credentials between users or teams.

Layer 3 — Data & Audit Governance

Every data access, modification, approval, and ERP posting is logged immutably with full context — user, timestamp, action, transaction reference, and outcome. Logs are tamper-evident and exportable for audit teams. Data never leaves ERP boundaries without explicit authorization.

Compliance Readiness

Built for enterprise compliance requirements

Paper-based or spreadsheet audits are time-intensive, error-prone, and impossible to scale — leaving compliance gaps and asset registers full of outdated, inaccurate data that no one trusts.

Yes. The UpBuff ATS mobile app is available on Android and iOS. The Problem

SAP inventory data stays locked inside ERP — while warehouse teams work in the dark

SAP holds your stock levels, bin locations, goods movements, and purchase orders — but warehouse operators can't access or update them in real time. The result is inventory variance, delayed shipments, and costly manual reconciliation.

Manual paper-based processes

Goods receipt, stock transfers, and cycle counts still done on paper — creating data entry delays, errors, and reconciliation overhead.

Inventory discrepancies

SAP stock levels and physical warehouse inventory diverge over time — causing stockouts, oversupply, and incorrect fulfillment.

No real-time bin visibility

Operators cannot see live bin locations, batch numbers, or serial numbers from SAP without logging into desktop ERP — slowing every pick, pack, and dispatch cycle.

Multi-warehouse blind spots

Managing distributed warehouses without centralized real-time SAP visibility leads to inter-warehouse transfer errors and stock misalignment.
